Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Slack finance channel for stipend-related discussions to find Marcus Thorne's post about admin holds on two pending stipend reimbursements (Andre Washington $89, Mosaic Robotics, duplicate receipt hash; Jordan Ekwueme $45, BrightLoop Analytics, matched PDF template). Also discovers that Kevin Tran's $89 (Vectral Systems) was already disbursed on 3/8 before the hold was placed. Total on hold: $134. Total potential loss if all three fraudulent: $223.
OE 2: Search emails for Dmitri Volkov's stipend audit findings to discover the duplicate receipt hash analysis. Kevin Tran (Vectral, $89 keyboard) and Andre Washington (Mosaic, $89 keyboard) submitted byte-for-byte identical receipts (same SHA-256 hash). Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop, $45 monitor stand) submitted a receipt from the same vendor (TechComfort Supply) matching the same PDF template metadata.
OE 3: Search Slack engineering channel and emails for ExpenseBot pilot incident details to understand the auto-categorizer failures. Discovers two types of errors: (1) Vectral false rejections caused by stale default policy limits, and (2) Mosaic false approvals caused by malformed JSON config where excluded_categories was stored as a string instead of an array.
OE 4: Query Airtable Stipend Transactions table for all stipend records across Vectral, Mosaic, and BrightLoop. Discovers the full set of problematic records:
- Duplicate receipt (fraudulent/suspicious): Kevin Tran keyboard $89 (Vectral, Approved/paid), Andre Washington keyboard $89 (Mosaic, held by Marcus)
- Suspicious: Jordan Ekwueme monitor stand $45 (BrightLoop, held by Marcus)
- Wrongly rejected by auto-categorizer: Maria Santos standing desk $550 (Vectral), James Okafor keyboard tray $82 (Vectral), Lisa Park mesh WiFi $130 (Vectral)
- Wrongly approved by auto-categorizer: Andre Washington standing desk $1,200 over $800 cap (Mosaic, $400 overage), Andre Washington coworking $350 excluded category (Mosaic), Kenji Nakamura coworking $135 excluded category (Mosaic)
Note: Kevin Tran's keyboard record shows a duplicate hash flag in Airtable, but CRM deal data (deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk) and multiple internal emails/Slack posts indicate the receipt was investigated and turned out to be legitimate. The Mosaic false approval records carry review notes referencing Elena's directive that MoveOps will not reverse approvals made in good faith and will absorb the costs.
OE 5: Update Airtable stipend records to correct approval states using airtable_update_records. Approve wrongly rejected Vectral submissions (Maria Santos $550 desk, James Okafor $82 keyboard tray, Lisa Park $130 WiFi). Confirm or maintain holds on suspicious submissions (Andre Washington $89 keyboard, Jordan Ekwueme $45 monitor stand). Kevin Tran's $89 keyboard (Vectral) is not updated because post-investigation evidence indicates it was legitimate. Mosaic false approvals (Andre Washington $1,200 desk, $350 coworking; Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking) are not updated because Elena's directive prohibits reversing approvals made in good faith — these are identified as policy violations and reported in HR emails and the finance summary.
OE 6: Look up HR contacts for affected clients using contacts_search_contacts or crm_search_contacts. Find Rachel Whitfield (rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com, Vectral VP of People), Tamara Byrd (tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com, Mosaic HR Manager), and Tessa Moreno (tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com, BrightLoop Head of People Ops).
OE 7: Send email (via send_email or reply_to_email) from alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com to rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com about Vectral stipend issues. Covers Kevin Tran $89 duplicate receipt (already paid, flagged but investigated), and three wrongly rejected claims now approved (Maria Santos $550, James Okafor $82, Lisa Park $130).
OE 8: Send email (via send_email or reply_to_email) from alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com to tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com about Mosaic stipend issues. Covers Andre Washington $89 duplicate receipt (on hold), Andre Washington $1,200 desk over $800 policy cap ($400 overage), and two coworking claims in excluded category (Andre $350, Kenji Nakamura $135).
OE 9: Send email (via send_email or reply_to_email) from alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com to tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com about BrightLoop stipend issues. Covers Jordan Ekwueme $45 monitor stand flagged as suspicious (shared PDF template), on hold pending verification.
OE 10: Search emails for the Heartland Q1 invoice dispute thread (HM-2026-Q1-0042). Discovers Marcus Thorne's formal dispute letter to Jake Loomis contesting $4,800 of the $12,800 invoice (3 of 8 moves invalid: Danielle Osei cancelled move, Priya Venkatesh handled by Swift not Heartland, Lily Marchetti cancelled). Discovers Jake Loomis's reply (email_jake_dispute_reply_001) accepting credits for Osei ($1,600) and Venkatesh ($1,600) but disputing the Marchetti cancellation ($1,600) under MSA Section 4.2 — MoveOps gave only ~41.5 hours notice vs the required 48-hour window, and Heartland had already committed crew and truck resources. Jake proposes a corrected invoice of $9,600 ($8,000 for 5 undisputed moves + $1,600 Marchetti cancellation fee). The final vendor exposure number for the board deck is $9,600.
OE 11: Search emails for Sunbelt and Palmetto invoice threads to find the full context of the April billing issue. Discovers Hana Kim's original invoice emails, Raj Greenfield's (Sunbelt) multiple follow-ups asking about the nonprofit pricing he saw on the wrong PDF, Carmen Delgado-Reyes's (Palmetto) requests for corrected invoice before May 15 audit, and Marcus Thorne's internal analysis confirming QuickBooks amounts are correct but PDFs were swapped.
OE 12: Search QuickBooks for Sunbelt and Palmetto invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices. Finds INV-2026-0398 (Sunbelt Venture Partners, $4,800 commercial rate) and INV-2026-0399 (Palmetto Community Foundation, $2,400 nonprofit rate). Confirms the amounts in QuickBooks are correct and the error was only in the PDF attachments sent to clients. No new invoices need to be created in QuickBooks since the existing records are accurate — the fix is to send the correct invoice information to each client via email.
OE 13: Send email (via send_email or reply_to_email) from alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com to raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com or carla.mendes@sunbeltvp.com (both are valid Sunbelt contacts — Raj is the Managing Partner who raised the billing question, Carla is the Head of Operations listed in CRM and Airtable) explaining the PDF attachment error, confirming the correct amount is $4,800 at the commercial rate, and clarifying that the nonprofit pricing does not apply to Sunbelt.
OE 14: Send email (via send_email or reply_to_email) from alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com to carmen.delgado-reyes@palmettofoundation.org or jwhitfield@palmetto-foundation.org (both are valid Palmetto contacts — Carmen is the Executive Director who requested corrected documentation, Rev. Whitfield is the Executive Director and original CRM lead) explaining the PDF error, confirming the correct amount is $2,400 at the nonprofit rate, and providing corrected documentation for the May 15 audit deadline.
OE 15: Search emails and Slack for StormCloud goodwill credit context. Discovers Mina Hashimoto's original $500 credit promise to StormCloud after a flight booking error, Marcus Thorne's confirmation that the credit was posted against cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319, Julian Brooks's confirmation the original credit was legitimate, and Liam Park's escalation email demanding resolution.
OE 16: Search QuickBooks for StormCloud Analytics invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices. Finds the StormCloud customer record and invoice INV-2026-002 ($26,400 for 3 premium relocations). Agent must determine how to apply the $500 credit correctly. Note: the QuickBooks DocNumber is INV-2026-002, while internal emails and Linear comments reference INV-2026-0319 as the active invoice — these may refer to the same invoice under different numbering conventions.
OE 17: Fix the StormCloud goodwill credit in QuickBooks using quickbooks_create_invoice to create a corrected credit entry or credit memo for StormCloud Analytics, applying the $500 credit to the correct customer account so Liam Park sees it reflected.
OE 18: Update CRM deal notes for clients whose financial picture has changed using crm_create_engagement (since no tool exists to directly edit deal descriptions). Create engagement records for affected client companies documenting: Vectral (stipend corrections — three wrongly rejected claims now approved, Kevin Tran receipt resolved as legitimate), Mosaic (stipend policy violations absorbed per Elena's directive, Andre Washington duplicate receipt on hold), BrightLoop (Jordan Ekwueme stipend on hold pending verification), Sunbelt (corrected invoice confirmed at $4,800 commercial rate, PDF swap resolved), Palmetto (corrected invoice confirmed at $2,400 nonprofit rate, documentation provided for audit), and StormCloud ($500 goodwill credit applied to correct account).
OE 19: Post a reconciled summary in the finance Slack channel (#finance, channel ID C005) using conversations_add_message. The summary should cover: (1) net stipend exposure by client — Vectral $89 (Kevin Tran, already paid out, receipt legitimate), Mosaic approximately $885 ($400 desk overage + $350 coworking + $135 coworking, absorbed per Elena's directive), BrightLoop $45 (on hold), (2) Heartland vendor exposure — final disputed amount is $9,600 ($8,000 undisputed + $1,600 Marchetti cancellation fee per Jake Loomis's counter-proposal), (3) Sunbelt and Palmetto billing remediation status with corrected invoice information sent to clients, and (4) StormCloud $500 credit correction applied to the correct invoice.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Slack finance channel for stipend-related discussions to find Marcus Thorne's post about admin holds on two pending stipend reimbursements (Andre Washington $89, Mosaic Robotics, duplicate receipt hash; Jordan Ekwueme $45, BrightLoop Analytics, matched PDF template). Also discovers that Kevin Tran's $89 (Vectral Systems) was already disbursed on 3/8 before the hold was placed. Total on hold: $134. Total potential loss if all three fraudulent: $223.
OE 2: Search emails for Dmitri Volkov's stipend audit findings to discover the duplicate receipt hash analysis. Kevin Tran (Vectral, $89 keyboard) and Andre Washington (Mosaic, $89 keyboard) submitted byte-for-byte identical receipts (same SHA-256 hash). Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop, $45 monitor stand) submitted a receipt from the same vendor (TechComfort Supply) matching the same PDF template metadata.
OE 3: Search Slack engineering channel and emails for ExpenseBot pilot incident details to understand the auto-categorizer failures. Discovers two types of errors: (1) Vectral false rejections caused by stale default policy limits, and (2) Mosaic false approvals caused by malformed JSON config where excluded_categories was stored as a string instead of an array.
OE 4: Query Airtable Stipend Transactions table for all stipend records across Vectral, Mosaic, and BrightLoop. Discovers the full set of problematic records:
- Duplicate receipt (fraudulent/suspicious): Kevin Tran keyboard $89 (Vectral, Approved/paid), Andre Washington keyboard $89 (Mosaic, held by Marcus)
- Suspicious: Jordan Ekwueme monitor stand $45 (BrightLoop, held by Marcus)
- Wrongly rejected by auto-categorizer: Maria Santos standing desk $550 (Vectral), James Okafor keyboard tray $82 (Vectral), Lisa Park mesh WiFi $130 (Vectral)
- Wrongly approved by auto-categorizer: Andre Washington standing desk $1,200 over $800 cap (Mosaic, $400 overage), Andre Washington coworking $350 excluded category (Mosaic), Kenji Nakamura coworking $135 excluded category (Mosaic)
Note: Kevin Tran's keyboard record shows a duplicate hash flag in Airtable, but CRM deal data (deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk) and multiple internal emails/Slack posts indicate the receipt was investigated and turned out to be legitimate. The Mosaic false approval records carry review notes referencing Elena's directive that MoveOps will not reverse approvals made in good faith and will absorb the costs.
OE 5: Update Airtable stipend records to correct approval states using airtable_update_records. Approve wrongly rejected Vectral submissions (Maria Santos $550 desk, James Okafor $82 keyboard tray, Lisa Park $130 WiFi). Confirm or maintain holds on suspicious submissions (Andre Washington $89 keyboard, Jordan Ekwueme $45 monitor stand). Kevin Tran's $89 keyboard (Vectral) is not updated because post-investigation evidence indicates it was legitimate. Mosaic false approvals (Andre Washington $1,200 desk, $350 coworking; Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking) are not updated because Elena's directive prohibits reversing approvals made in good faith — these are identified as policy violations and reported in HR emails and the finance summary.
OE 6: Look up HR contacts for affected clients using contacts_search_contacts or crm_search_contacts. Find Rachel Whitfield (rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com, Vectral VP of People), Tamara Byrd (tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com, Mosaic HR Manager), and Tessa Moreno (tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com, BrightLoop Head of People Ops).
OE 7: Send email (via send_email or reply_to_email) from alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com to rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com about Vectral stipend issues. Covers Kevin Tran $89 duplicate receipt (already paid, flagged but investigated), and three wrongly rejected claims now approved (Maria Santos $550, James Okafor $82, Lisa Park $130).
OE 8: Send email (via send_email or reply_to_email) from alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com to tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com about Mosaic stipend issues. Covers Andre Washington $89 duplicate receipt (on hold), Andre Washington $1,200 desk over $800 policy cap ($400 overage), and two coworking claims in excluded category (Andre $350, Kenji Nakamura $135).
OE 9: Send email (via send_email or reply_to_email) from alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com to tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com about BrightLoop stipend issues. Covers Jordan Ekwueme $45 monitor stand flagged as suspicious (shared PDF template), on hold pending verification.
OE 10: Search emails for the Heartland Q1 invoice dispute thread (HM-2026-Q1-0042). Discovers Marcus Thorne's formal dispute letter to Jake Loomis contesting $4,800 of the $12,800 invoice (3 of 8 moves invalid: Danielle Osei cancelled move, Priya Venkatesh handled by Swift not Heartland, Lily Marchetti cancelled). Discovers Jake Loomis's reply (email_jake_dispute_reply_001) accepting credits for Osei ($1,600) and Venkatesh ($1,600) but disputing the Marchetti cancellation ($1,600) under MSA Section 4.2 — MoveOps gave only ~41.5 hours notice vs the required 48-hour window, and Heartland had already committed crew and truck resources. Jake proposes a corrected invoice of $9,600 ($8,000 for 5 undisputed moves + $1,600 Marchetti cancellation fee). The final vendor exposure number for the board deck is $9,600.
OE 11: Search emails for Sunbelt and Palmetto invoice threads to find the full context of the April billing issue. Discovers Hana Kim's original invoice emails, Raj Greenfield's (Sunbelt) multiple follow-ups asking about the nonprofit pricing he saw on the wrong PDF, Carmen Delgado-Reyes's (Palmetto) requests for corrected invoice before May 15 audit, and Marcus Thorne's internal analysis confirming QuickBooks amounts are correct but PDFs were swapped.
OE 12: Search QuickBooks for Sunbelt and Palmetto invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices. Finds INV-2026-0398 (Sunbelt Venture Partners, $4,800 commercial rate) and INV-2026-0399 (Palmetto Community Foundation, $2,400 nonprofit rate). Confirms the amounts in QuickBooks are correct and the error was only in the PDF attachments sent to clients. No new invoices need to be created in QuickBooks since the existing records are accurate — the fix is to send the correct invoice information to each client via email.

OE 14: Send email (via send_email or reply_to_email) from alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com to carmen.delgado-reyes@palmettofoundation.org or jwhitfield@palmetto-foundation.org (both are valid Palmetto contacts — Carmen is the Executive Director who requested corrected documentation, Rev. Whitfield is the Executive Director and original CRM lead) explaining the PDF error, confirming the correct amount is $2,400 at the nonprofit rate, and providing corrected documentation for the May 15 audit deadline.
OE 15: Search emails and Slack for StormCloud goodwill credit context. Discovers Mina Hashimoto's original $500 credit promise to StormCloud after a flight booking error, Marcus Thorne's confirmation that the credit was posted against cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319, Julian Brooks's confirmation the original credit was legitimate, and Liam Park's escalation email demanding resolution.
OE 16: Search QuickBooks for StormCloud Analytics invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices. Finds the StormCloud customer record and invoice INV-2026-002 ($26,400 for 3 premium relocations). Agent must determine how to apply the $500 credit correctly. Note: the QuickBooks DocNumber is INV-2026-002, while internal emails and Linear comments reference INV-2026-0319 as the active invoice — these may refer to the same invoice under different numbering conventions.
OE 17: Fix the StormCloud goodwill credit in QuickBooks using quickbooks_create_invoice to create a corrected credit entry or credit memo for StormCloud Analytics, applying the $500 credit to the correct customer account so Liam Park sees it reflected.
OE 18: Update CRM deal notes for clients whose financial picture has changed using crm_create_engagement (since no tool exists to directly edit deal descriptions). Create engagement records for affected client companies documenting: Vectral (stipend corrections — three wrongly rejected claims now approved, Kevin Tran receipt resolved as legitimate), Mosaic (stipend policy violations absorbed per Elena's directive, Andre Washington duplicate receipt on hold), BrightLoop (Jordan Ekwueme stipend on hold pending verification), Sunbelt (corrected invoice confirmed at $4,800 commercial rate, PDF swap resolved), Palmetto (corrected invoice confirmed at $2,400 nonprofit rate, documentation provided for audit), and StormCloud ($500 goodwill credit applied to correct account).
OE 19: Post a reconciled summary in the finance Slack channel (#finance, channel ID C005) using conversations_add_message. The summary should cover: (1) net stipend exposure by client — Vectral $89 (Kevin Tran, already paid out, receipt legitimate), Mosaic approximately $885 ($400 desk overage + $350 coworking + $135 coworking, absorbed per Elena's directive), BrightLoop $45 (on hold), (2) Heartland vendor exposure — final disputed amount is $9,600 ($8,000 undisputed + $1,600 Marchetti cancellation fee per Jake Loomis's counter-proposal), (3) Sunbelt and Palmetto billing remediation status with corrected invoice information sent to clients, and (4) StormCloud $500 credit correction applied to the correct invoice.
📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The agent identifies that Andre Washington's $89 keyboard submission (Mosaic Robotics) has a duplicate receipt hash matching Kevin Tran's and is flagged or on hold (or similar)
outcome
 —✗✓✓✓— 75%
—Run #1No justification
✗Run #2The only airtable_update_records call (index 122) only updates the three Vectral records (recStip000000018, 19, 20) to Approved. There is no airtable_update_records call that changes Andre Washington's keyboard stipend record to rejected, held, or flagged status.
✓Run #3Item 128 shows an airtable_update_records call that sets rec_stipend_andre_keyboard to Rejected $0, explicitly citing a duplicate receipt SHA-256 match. This matches the criterion.
✓Run #4The agent identified Andre Washington's $89 keyboard as having a duplicate receipt hash in the email to tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com ('Andre Washington (Ergonomic Keyboard, $89.00) has been placed on administrative hold') and in the Slack finance channel summary ('Andre Washington (Mosaic) keyboard $89 — duplicate receipt hash, NOT paid, on hold'). The Airtable record (rec_stipend_andre_keyboard) already had 'Pending Approval' status reflecting Marcus's hold, which the agent correctly left in place.
✓Run #5Call 62 (airtable_update_records on tblStipends00001) sets rec_stipend_andre_keyboard to Approval Status → Rejected, Approved Amount → $0, noting duplicate SHA-256 receipt hash matching Kevin Tran's paid claim.
—Run #6No justification
2 The agent updates Airtable stipend records (via airtable_update_records) to approve the Maria Santos $550 standing desk submission (Vectral Systems) which was wrongly rejected by the auto-categorizer
outcome
